visuals.title: Decision Tree Induction: Structure, Splitting Criteria, and Algorithms
visuals.description: Animated diagram showing the decision tree structure with root, decision, and leaf nodes, the two main splitting criteria (Entropy-based Information Gain and Gini Impurity), the three major induction algorithms (ID3, C4.5, CART) with their key differences, and the overall tree-building process including pruning strategies.

nodes.label: Start with Entire Dataset at Root Node
For each candidate feature, calculate splitting criterion (e.g., IG, Gini)
Select feature with best value for criterion
Split data into subsets based on feature value
Create a new internal node for the split
For each subset, check stopping criteria
If STOP: make Leaf Node with predicted class/value
